label: Statuspage
description: Manage Atlassian Statuspage pages, incidents, and components to communicate service health to your users.

form.title: Connect Statuspage
form.subtitle: Paste your Statuspage API key to let Anton read and update your status pages and incidents.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste your personal Statuspage API key. Generated in seconds from your User Profile — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Statuspage API Key

Statuspage uses a personal API key tied to your user account. Any pages your account can access will be available to Anton.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Statuspage](https://manage.statuspage.io).
2. Click your avatar or name in the top-right corner and choose **Profile**.
3. Scroll to the **API Info** section.
4. Copy the value shown under **API key** (it looks like a long alphanumeric string, e.g. `a1b2c3d4e5f6...`).
5. Paste it into the **API Key** field below.
6. Optionally enter a specific **Page ID** to scope Anton to one page, or leave it blank to work across all pages your account can see.

### Notes

- The API key is scoped to your Statuspage user account. If your account is removed or the key is regenerated, you will need to re-enter it here.
- To find a **Page ID**, go to **Manage → Pages**, select a page, and look at the URL: `manage.statuspage.io/pages/<page_id>`.
- Organization admins can see all pages; restricted users see only pages they have been granted access to.
- Treat this key like a password — anyone with it can create and resolve incidents on your behalf.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Statuspage → User Profile → API Info. Treat it like a password.

fields.name: page_id
fields.label: Page ID (optional)
fields.description: Scope Anton to a single Statuspage page. Find it in the URL when viewing a page in the dashboard: manage.statuspage.io/pages/<page_id>. Leave blank to access all pages.
